understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each level within a limited step budget by maneuvering the selectable piece(s) around obstacles—often by pushing blocks—and ultimately getting a selectable onto the goal tile(s) (shown as '+') to complete the level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Positions were given as pixel coordinates on a grid where each move shifted the selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction. If a pushable block was immediately adjacent in the move direction, the move could push it along, provided the space beyond was not blocked. Walls acted as impassable or interaction-defining boundaries; the movement_blocked flags were only hints indicating adjacency and did not guarantee the move would fail. Some levels contained multiple selectable pieces; using CLICK on another selectable’s exact position switched control to that piece without moving it.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each level within a limited step budget by maneuvering the selectable piece(s) around obstacles—often by pushing blocks—and ultimately getting a selectable onto the goal tile(s) (shown as '+') to complete the level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Positions were given as pixel coordinates on a grid where each move shifted the selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction. If a pushable block was immediately adjacent in the move direction, the move could push it along, provided the space beyond was not blocked. Walls acted as impassable or interaction-defining boundaries; the movement_blocked flags were only hints indicating adjacency and did not guarantee the move would fail. Some levels contained multiple selectable pieces; using CLICK on another selectable’s exact position switched control to that piece without moving it.
